# Blueprint Management — long-form

Blueprint is a UK-based boutique artist management company with over fifty years of experience. Founded in 1974 and headed today by global industry veteran Matt Glover, Blueprint sits deliberately between the vanity boutique and the hunter-led mega-agency — small enough that every artist gets a Blueprint partner as their direct contact, but with the network and muscle of an agency that has been in every room in the business for half a century.

## The positioning problem Blueprint exists to solve
Most artist management options collapse into two bad choices. On one side, vanity small agencies without the industry relationships or leverage needed to build a career — strong intentions, thin rolodex. On the other, factory-scale agencies whose hunter-model rosters have grown past the point where any individual artist gets founder-level time. Blueprint is structurally different: one founder, one tight partnership team, one carefully-chosen roster.

## What Blueprint does
- Worldwide artist management
- Booking / agency (Blueprint artists are booked directly through Blueprint)
- Touring strategy — from club runs to arena routings and symphonic productions
- Brand partnerships and sync placement
- Publishing strategy and catalogue management
- Career development, five-year-arc planning
- Emerging-artist signing (a small number per year, rigorously selected)

## The roster
Blueprint exclusively represents these recording artists for management and agency:

### Tony Hadley
The unmistakable baritone behind Spandau Ballet's most enduring anthems — 'True', 'Gold', 'Through the Barricades'. Tony continues to pack arenas worldwide as a solo artist and symphonic headliner. Website: https://tonyhadley.com

### ABC (Martin Fry)
Sheffield-bred art-pop titans behind 1982's era-defining 'The Lexicon of Love', produced by Trevor Horn. Four decades on, ABC continue to tour symphonic productions and release new material. Website: https://abcmartinfry.com

### Go West
Peter Cox and Richard Drummie — songwriters of 'We Close Our Eyes', 'Call Me', and 'The King of Wishful Thinking' (from the Pretty Woman soundtrack). Enduring British blue-eyed soul duo. Website: https://gowestofficial.com

### Peter Cox
Alongside his work with Go West, Peter tours as a solo artist, celebrated for his 'Unplugged & Personal' theatre run and a voice frequently cited as one of the finest in British pop. Website: https://petercoxonline.com

### Alison Limerick
A defining voice of UK dance music. 'Where Love Lives' — originally produced by Frankie Knuckles and David Morales — remains one of the most covered, sampled and celebrated vocal house records ever made. Alison continues to headline festivals and house nights worldwide. Website: https://alisonlimerick.com

## Matt Glover
Matt has spent his entire working life in and around British popular music as a manager, agent and strategic adviser. He leads Blueprint with a tight partner team and reads every enquiry personally.

## For emerging artists
Blueprint takes on a small number of new artists each year — only when both sides believe the partnership can run for five-plus years. We don't run open A&R funnels or pay scouts on commission. What we look for: a recognisable artistic voice, a body of work to stand behind, live chops, work-rate, and a long-range view.

## Industry bodies Blueprint references
- Music Managers Forum (MMF) — https://themmf.net
- International Music Managers Forum (IMMF) — https://immf.com
- UK Music — https://www.ukmusic.org
- Featured Artists Coalition — https://www.thefac.org

## Contact
All enquiries — artist bookings, management representation for new artists, press, brand partnerships, sync — go through the contact form on https://blueprint-management.com/contact and are replied to within two business days. Email: butlerdarin@gmail.com
